texts.label: text
texts.orig: Lithium and its compounds have more extensive application for their special physical and chemical properties compared with other metal elements. They have been widely applied in lithium battery field which can be used in various communication equipment and environment-friendly vehicles, and a kind of fuel for fusion reactor [1-3]. With the rapid development of the world industry, the demands for lithium resources increase, and the consumption of lithium products in various areas grows continually and rapidly [4]. Therefore, only if some cost-effective technologies for lithium recovery were developed, could the scarce of lithium resources be relieved.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An electrodialysis (ED) system equipped with monovalent selective permeability ion exchange membrane was used to investigate the separation of magnesium and lithium in salt lake brines. Some objective functions including separation coefficient of magnesium and lithium (FMg A Li), recovery ratio of lithium (RLi) and current efficiency ( g ), were applied to explore the effect of the applied voltage, the linear flow velocities, the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio and pH on the process of extracting lithium. The results showed that the increase of applied voltage could improve the separation coefficient and the recovery ratio of lithium, but energy consumption would also rise; with the increase of feed linear velocity, the separation coefficient had a rising trend, but too large linear velocity would lead to increasing energy consumption and reducing current efficiency. When the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was in the range of 5-92, ED technology showed higher separation efficiency. Linear flow velocities of concentrating and desalting compartments (1.9-7.6 cm/s) showed positive correlation with the migration of the ions. According to the comprehensive analysis of the experimental results, the optimal operation conditions of the ED device are as follows: the applied voltage (5 V), concentration of electrolyte solution (5 wt% Na2SO4) and the linear velocities of desalting/concentrating/electrolyte (6.2, 6.2 and 3.8 cm/s). When the brine with Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 60 was treated by the ED for 2 h under this condition, the separation coefficient was 12.48. And the recovery ratio of Li + , current efficiency and the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of concentrate was 72.46 wt%, 8.68 wt% and 7, respectively. The concentrate, which could be considered as a bittern with lower Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (<8), will be easy to be treated in the subsequent process.

texts.orig: Lithium resources in salt lakes account for about 90% of total proven reserves of the onshore lithium resources in the world. These salt lakes are mainly distributed in Bolivia, Argentina, Chile, China and the United states [5]. The lithium concentration, magnesium concentration and Mg 2+ /Li + ratio in some known brine deposits all over the world was listed in Table 1 [6,7]. Large-scale industrialized production of lithium mainly appeared in three lakes: Atacama (Chile), Hombre Muerto (Argentina) and Silver peak (U.S.) [8-10]. The precipitation method [9,11] has been developed to separate and recover lithium from above three salt lake brines with a low Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (according to the suggestion of Yu et al. [7], the brine is low Mg 2+ /Li + ratio when the mass value of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio is below 8, others are high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio). But the loss of some lithium salt makes it unsuitable for the majority of global salt lake brines which belong to the type of high magnesium and low lithium [12]. texts.label: text
texts.orig: As an important membrane separation technology, ED has been used in many fields, especially in seawater and brackish water desalination and some certain separation fields [22-26]. ED gets more and more attention for water utilization and metal ions recovery. In the previous work, the lithium resource recovery from brine with the high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 60 by using electrodialysis (ED) has been studied. However, the magnesium in the brine was precipitated by use of the lime and the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was decreased to 5 before extracting lithium [27]. Furthermore, Hoshino [28,29] developed a novel technology for recovering lithium from seawater by electrodialysis using ionic liquid membrane. The method involves an ionic-liquid-impregnated organic membrane, which only the Li + ions in seawater and not the other ions, including Na + , Mg 2+ , Ca 2+ and K + , permeate from the anode side to the cathode side in ED process. Then, the Li + become concentrated on the cathode side and can be recovered. Extracting lithium from brine through combining electrodialysis (ED) with ion sieve adsorption method has been researched [30]. This method is a valuable technology which is relatively economical and environment-friendly, and it's also the key research direction in the future for extracting lithium from salt lake brines with high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As one of the technologies that give a possibility to separate monovalent ions from brine and seawater [31], ED equipped with monovalent selective ion exchange membranes was used to separate the magnesium and lithium from high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio brine in this study. ED is a technique based on the transport of ions through selective membranes under an electrical field [32]. In the process of ion migration, the ion can pass through a membrane when it has opposite charge to fixed charge of membrane, otherwise the ion can't pass. So the ions concentration in concentrating compartment increases with the decline of the one in desalting compartment. So, concentrated solution and desalted liquid can be obtained. Then the separation and purification of certain ions can be achieved. Separation of sodium ions from trivalent chromium of tanning waste water by ED using monovalent cation selective membranes has been researched [33]. Moreover, monovalent selective ion exchange membranes usually have high selectivity towards lithium, but they were determined to have extremely low selectivity towards magnesium. So, it can decrease the ratio of magnesium and lithium in brine, and at the same time the lithium ions were concentrated in the concentrating compartment. This was also provided convenience for subsequent preparation of the lithium products. The applied voltage, linear velocity of desalting and concentrating cells, Mg 2+ /Li + ratio and pH value were investigated in this separation process of magnesium and lithium. The optimal operating conditions of ED were obtained through the analysis of separation coefficient, recovery ratio and current efficiency. Meanwhile, the specific energy consumption was also discussed.

texts.orig: The schematic diagram of ED process and migration of ions in membrane stack is depicted in Fig. 1. The experimental apparatus mainly consisted of dilute solution tank, concentrate solution tank, electrolyte solution tank, direct-current (DC) power unit, membrane stack and tube pump. The membrane stack was consisted of ten pairs (ACS and CIMS). The size of membrane was 300 mm -100 mm, and its effective membrane area of each pair was 140.7 cm 2 . The spacer thickness was 0.90 mm.

texts.orig: The internal structure of ED membrane stack is shown in Fig. 2. The stack is comprised of fixation plate, anode plate, cation exchange membrane, anion exchange membrane, concentrating cell spacer, and desalting cell spacer and cathode plate. Diagonal nets spacers are incorporated into the concentrating and desalting cells.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The process of this experiment was conducted in batch mode at room temperature. The desalting compartment and concentrating compartment were filled with 2.5 L of artificial brine with high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (chlorine salt system) and 1.5 L of a certain concentration of sodium chloride solution, respectively. In addition, the electrolyte compartment was filled with 2.5 L of 5 wt% Na2SO4 solution. At the beginning of the experiment, the artificial brine, NaCl solution and Na2SO4 solution were put into the dilute solution tank, concentrate solution tank and electrolyte solution tank, respectively. Cathode compartment shared the same electrolyte solution tank with anode compartment, and the electrolyte solution was transported by the same pump. This technology made the lithium, which entered in the cathode compartment through the cathode membrane, return to the membrane stack through the anode membrane from anode compartment. So, the lithium loss could be reduced in the whole process. Then the booster pumps feed these solutions into the respective compartments. The linear flow velocity of the electrolyte compartment was 3.8 cm/s (flow rate 80 L/h) during the whole experiment. All experiments were run for 120 min, and samples were taken every 20 min. The concentration of Li + was determined by AAS (Atomic Absorption Spectrometry), and Mg 2+ and Cl � were analyzed by EDTA titration method and ''Silver Nitrate Titration' (ASTM D512-89 (1999) ''Standard Test Methods for Chloride Ion in Water'), respectively. The amount of Na + was calculated by charge balance method in the solution.

texts.orig: The variation of separation coefficient and recovery ratio with the operation time under different applied voltages was shown in Fig. 3. The separation coefficient and recovery ratio of lithium were expressed by FMg A Li and RLi, respectively. As shown in Fig. 3 (a), the FMg A Li increased with the extension of operation time, but the growth rate under different applied voltages was inconsistent. The FMg A Li value increased quickly as the operating time at 56 V, but slowly at 2-3 V. The migration rate of lithium showed a positive relationship with the voltage, so higher applied voltage induces higher FMg A Li value. But the voltage shouldn't be increased unboundedly because of concentration polarization phenomenon under higher voltage. This phenomenon was not only harmful to FMg A Li, but also to ED process. Fig. 3(b) illustrated the RLi increased with the rise of voltage in the first 100 min of these experiments. When the applied voltage was 5 V and 6 V, the RLi value could achieve the maximum number (75.44%) after 100 min. It indicated that FMg A Li values were much different under 5 V and 6 V, but the RLi values were trend to equivalent at the end of experiments. On the other hand, the RLi value maintained a lower level when the applied voltages were 2 V and 3 V, which was due to ions permeate fluxes at a low level under lower voltage. When the applied voltage was 4 V, the RLi increased linearly within 80 min, and then the growth rate became slow. It may be attributed to the declining of Li + migration rate in the desalting compartment due to the slow decrease of the current density at the later stage of operation.

It indicated that FMg A Li values were much different under 5 V and 6 V, but the RLi values were trend to equivalent at the end of experiments. On the other hand, the RLi value maintained a lower level when the applied voltages were 2 V and 3 V, which was due to ions permeate fluxes at a low level under lower voltage. When the applied voltage was 4 V, the RLi increased linearly within 80 min, and then the growth rate became slow. It may be attributed to the declining of Li + migration rate in the desalting compartment due to the slow decrease of the current density at the later stage of operation.

texts.orig: The linear flow velocities of desalting and concentrating compartments were other important factors in the circulatory experiment. And the artificial brine composition was similar to the above. When the electrolyte linear flow velocity was kept at 3.8 cm/s, five different linear flow velocities (1.9, 3.3, 4.8, 6.2, 7.60 cm/s, i.e., flow rates of 40, 70, 100, 130, 160 L/h, accordingly), were investigated at room temperature, Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 60 and applied voltage of 5 V. The results were shown in Figs. 6 and 7. It can be found that both FMg A Li and RLi increased relatively with the rise of linear velocity. FMg A Li value after 120 min was increased with the rise of linear velocity. The main reason was that the turbulent extent and uniform distribution of the solution in desalting compartment and concentrating compartment became better with the increase of linear velocity. These hydrodynamic factors had a beneficial effect on reducing the boundary layer thickness of membrane surface and speeding up the migration velocity of lithium ions [34]. So, the higher linear velocity was, the higher FMg A Li value was.

texts.orig: The variation of current and current efficiency with time in different linear flow velocities was shown in Fig. 7. It could be seen from Fig. 7(a) that the current curves kept a downward tendency with time. And as shown in the Fig. 7(b), under the same applied voltage (5 V), the current efficiency ( g ) decreased gradually with time. This was mainly because that the amount of Li + migration would be reduced with the decrease of Li + concentration in desalting compartment and current density in desalting cell. The maximum of g can be obtained after 30 min when the linear flow velocity was 6.2 cm/s, and it reached the peak value of 9.35% at 80 min. The g value remained in the lower value before 70 min in the linear velocity of 1.9 cm/s and 3.3 cm/s. It could be illustrated that the interface stagnation layer between membrane and solution became thin due to the lower linear velocity. This phenomenon led to the total resistance increase and the lithium ions migration rate decrease.

texts.orig: Effect of the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio on the separation coefficient and the recovery ratio were given in Fig. 9. As can be seen from Fig. 9(a), the FMg A Li reached the maximum value of 51.07 at Mg 2+ /Li + = 37. While the FMg A Li value was only 5.39 when the Mg 2+ /Li + value was 5. And it was almost the same at 77 and 92. All the above turned out that lithium recovery by ED process was influenced by Mg 2+ /Li + value in the higher Mg 2+ /Li + ratio solution. Furthermore, the RLi value increased from 63.88% to 71.42% after raising Mg 2+ /Li + ratio from 5 to 92. This result seemed to show that the RLi value could be controlled by Mg 2+ /Li + ratio and explained by electro-migration and mass-transfer theory [36]. The ion deficit at the interface of solution and membrane phase in desalting cell was caused by the ion transference number in membrane phase greater than the number in solution during the ED process. This principle led to the decrease of current density in membrane phase under constant voltage, which had a disadvantage of the migration of lithium ions. So, to some extent, the RLi could be improved through adding magnesium.

It was apparent that the ESEC value was lower for the brine with lower Mg 2+ /Li + value, and it was in a high-level range for the brine with high Mg 2+ /Li + value. But it was not to say that the higher the Mg 2+ /Li + value, the larger the ESEC value. The ESEC curve at Mg 2+ /Li + = 92 was below the curves at the Mg 2+ /Li + value ranging from 51 to 77. This was mainly due to leakage of Mg 2+ from desalting compartment to concentrating compartment when the higher concentration of Mg 2+ was in artificial brine. And this tendency could lead to the decrease of the migration amount of Li + . So the ESEC value was ideal for a certain Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of brine.

Then the brine with a Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 60 was treated by the ED under the optimal condition, and the change of ions concentration in the desalting and concentrating compartment was shown in Table 3. The concentration of Li + was concentrated to 0.210 g/L from 0.148 g/L, and the FMg A Li was 12.48; the RLi and the g value were 72.46% and 8.68%, respectively. It could be seen that the value of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was decreased from 60 to 7 after 2 h. So the brine with high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (e.g., 60) could be translated to lower Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (<8) solution with the ED process, and its concentration of Li + was concentrated simultaneously. The Li + -enriched solution was convenient for further treatment with traditional methods, and then lithium products were obtained.

texts.text: A feasible ED process for enriching lithium from brines was proposed and investigated in this work. With exploring the effects of the applied voltage, the linear flow velocity, the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio, and pH on separation of lithium, the following conclusions were made:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (1) Applied voltage was a key factor affecting separation coefficient (FMg A Li), recovery ratio (RLi) and current efficiency ( g ); applying higher levels of voltage could increase the effects on the Li + migration rate in the desalting compartment, the FMg A Li and the RLi; in the view of the separation efficiency and economic benefit, the optimal applied voltage of the ED device was 5 V, and the RLi was 75.44% in this condition.
texts.text: Applied voltage was a key factor affecting separation coefficient (FMg A Li), recovery ratio (RLi) and current efficiency ( g ); applying higher levels of voltage could increase the effects on the Li + migration rate in the desalting compartment, the FMg A Li and the RLi; in the view of the separation efficiency and economic benefit, the optimal applied voltage of the ED device was 5 V, and the RLi was 75.44% in this condition.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (2) Along with the increase of the feed linear velocity, the FMg A Li showed an increasing trend. Feed linear velocity was positively correlated with the RLi, but it was unfavorable to use excessive feed flow in the experiment process, which would result in a mechanical damage to membranes and an additional burden on the pumps. The optimal linear velocity was 6.2 cm/s which had higher g and RLi value.
texts.text: Along with the increase of the feed linear velocity, the FMg A Li showed an increasing trend. Feed linear velocity was positively correlated with the RLi, but it was unfavorable to use excessive feed flow in the experiment process, which would result in a mechanical damage to membranes and an additional burden on the pumps. The optimal linear velocity was 6.2 cm/s which had higher g and RLi value.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (3) The FMg A Li and the RLi under Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 5 were lower than others. The RLi reached the highest value of 71.42% at the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 92. But the FMg A Li decreased with the increase of the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio when the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio ranged in 37-92. It indicated that ED method had a certain separation performance of magnesium and lithium for the high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio brines.
texts.text: The FMg A Li and the RLi under Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 5 were lower than others. The RLi reached the highest value of 71.42% at the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of 92. But the FMg A Li decreased with the increase of the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio when the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio ranged in 37-92. It indicated that ED method had a certain separation performance of magnesium and lithium for the high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio brines.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (4) The optimal pH for recovering lithium from high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio brines by ED was about 4-5 which had superior values of FMg A Li , RLi and g . And the specific energy consumption (ESEC) increased with the rise of applied voltage, linear velocity and Mg 2+ /Li + mass ratio. But generally speaking the lower pH was, the higher ESEC was about.
texts.text: The optimal pH for recovering lithium from high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio brines by ED was about 4-5 which had superior values of FMg A Li , RLi and g . And the specific energy consumption (ESEC) increased with the rise of applied voltage, linear velocity and Mg 2+ /Li + mass ratio. But generally speaking the lower pH was, the higher ESEC was about.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the optimal condition, the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of artificial brine was reduced from 60 to 7 by this ED process after 2 h, which provided an ideal Mg 2+ /Li + condition for the further extraction of lithium from the Li + -enriched solution. In short, ED was a prospective method in the recovery lithium from brines containing lithium, especially for the brine with high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. However, in order to obtain lithium salt like LiCl effectively, it is necessary to further increase the concentration of Li + and decrease the energy consumption. And the mixed salt system which contains both chloride and sulfate will be investigated in next step.

And the mixed salt system which contains both chloride and sulfate will be investigated in next step.
